search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk NG145 hyperthyroidism monitoring TSH FT4 FT3 follow-up
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic antithyroid medicine, radioiodine or surgery plan
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Results require clinician interpretation in treatment and population context.
No therapy, dose, procedure or referral is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults, children and young people with established hyperthyroidism; pregnancy and thyroid cancer require separate guidance.
setting_applicability: Primary or specialist outpatient hyperthyroidism follow-up.
UAE_applicability: NICE monitoring domains transfer partially; UAE laboratory ranges, medicines, pregnancy, specialist and definitive-treatment pathways govern.
recency_verification: Current NICE NG145 recommendations and 2025 surveillance status were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: NG145 remains current and was last updated in 2023.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact hyperthyroidism monitoring section supports interval change, symptoms, clinician-reviewed thyroid results, specialist correspondence, entered plan and arranged follow-up without changing medicine or inferring control.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-reviewed TSH, FT4, and FT3 monitoring, treatment-context review, and clinician-led follow-up or specialist advice without mapping medicine doses or autonomous treatment.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact information and warning-symptom section supports patient questions and safety-net documentation only when discussed by the clinician.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports shared decision documentation, thyroid-medicine interaction information, and specific thyroid-enlargement warning symptoms including shortness of breath, rapid growth, hoarse voice, and swallowing difficulty.

unresolved_source_gaps: Cause, symptoms, thyroid examination, eye disease, rhythm, exact results, medicine, adherence, adverse effects, pregnancy, interpretation and treatment remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
